items.title: xAI Launches Standalone Grok Speech-to-Text and Text-to-Speech APIs, Targeting Enterprise Voice Developers
items.summary:
Elon Musk’s AI company xAI has launched two standalone audio APIs — a Speech-to-Text (STT) API and a Text-to-Speech (TTS) API — both built on the same infrastructure that powers Grok Voice on mobile apps, Tesla vehicles, and Starlink customer support. The release moves xAI squarely into the competitive speech API market currently occupied by […]
The post xAI Launches Standalone Grok Speech-to-Text and Text-to-Speech APIs, Targeting Enterprise Voice Developers appeared first on MarkTechPost.

items.title: NVIDIA Releases Ising: the First Open Quantum AI Model Family for Hybrid Quantum-Classical Systems
items.summary:
Quantum computing has spent years living in the future tense. Hardware has improved, research has compounded, and venture dollars have followed — but the gap between a quantum processor running in a lab and one running a real-world application remains stubbornly wide. NVIDIA moved to close that gap with the launch of NVIDIA Ising, the […]
The post NVIDIA Releases Ising: the First Open Quantum AI Model Family for Hybrid Quantum-Classical Systems appeared first on MarkTechPost.

items.title: Meet OpenMythos: An Open-Source PyTorch Reconstruction of Claude Mythos Where 770M Parameters Match a 1.3B Transformer
items.summary:
Anthropic has never published a technical paper on Claude Mythos. That has not stopped the research community from theorizing. A new open-source project called OpenMythos, released on GitHub by Kye Gomez, attempts something ambitious: a first-principles theoretical reconstruction of what the Claude Mythos architecture might actually be, built entirely in PyTorch and grounded in peer-reviewed […]
The post Meet OpenMythos: An Open-Source PyTorch Reconstruction of Claude Mythos Where 770M Parameters Match a 1.3B Transformer appeared first on MarkTechPost.

items.title: The 12-month window
items.summary: A lot of AI startups exist partly because the foundation models haven't expanded into their category yet. As many jokingly acknowledge, that won't last forever.

items.title: A Coding Implementation to Build an AI-Powered File Type Detection and Security Analysis Pipeline with Magika and OpenAI
items.summary:
In this tutorial, we build a workflow that combines Magika’s deep-learning-based file type detection with OpenAI’s language intelligence to create a practical and insightful analysis pipeline. We begin by setting up the required libraries, securely connecting to the OpenAI API, and initializing Magika to classify files directly from raw bytes rather than relying on filenames […]
The post A Coding Implementation to Build an AI-Powered File Type Detection and Security Analysis Pipeline with Magika and OpenAI appeared first on MarkTechPost.

items.title: A Coding Tutorial for Running PrismML Bonsai 1-Bit LLM on CUDA with GGUF, Benchmarking, Chat, JSON, and RAG
items.summary:
In this tutorial, we implement how to run the Bonsai 1-bit large language model efficiently using GPU acceleration and PrismML’s optimized GGUF deployment stack. We set up the environment, install the required dependencies, and download the prebuilt llama.cpp binaries, and load the Bonsai-1.7B model for fast inference on CUDA. As we progress, we examine how […]
The post A Coding Tutorial for Running PrismML Bonsai 1-Bit LLM on CUDA with GGUF, Benchmarking, Chat, JSON, and RAG appeared first on MarkTechPost.

items.title: A verified subset of 500 software engineering problems from real GitHub issues, validated by human annotators for evaluating language models' ability to resolve real-world coding issues by generating patches for Python codebases.
Google is in talks with Marvell to build custom AI inference chips as it diversifies beyond Broadcom
items.summary:
Summary: Google is in talks with Marvell Technology to develop two new AI chips – a memory processing unit and an inference-optimised TPU – adding a third design partner alongside Broadcom and MediaTek in its custom silicon supply chain. The discussions, which have not yet produced a signed contract, came days after Broadcom locked in […]
This story continues at The Next Web

items.title: Cloud development platform Vercel was hacked
items.summary: Vercel, a major development platform that hosts and deploys web apps, was compromised, and the hackers are attempting to sell stolen data. A person claiming to be a member of ShinyHunters, which was behind the recent hack of Rockstar Games, posted some data online, including employee names, email addresses, and activity time stamps. Vercel confirmed […]

items.title: Meta targets 20 May for 8,000 layoffs as it redirects billions toward AI infrastructure
items.summary:
In short: Meta will begin companywide layoffs on 20 May, cutting approximately 8,000 employees (10% of its 78,865-person workforce), with additional cuts planned for the second half of 2026. The restructuring, which follows earlier rounds that brought Zuckerberg’s total cuts since 2022 to roughly 25,000, is driven by a reallocation toward AI infrastructure costing $115-135 […]
This story continues at The Next Web

items.title: Threads is redesigning its website and finally adding direct messages to the desktop
items.summary:
Summary: Threads head Connor Hayes previewed a redesigned web interface that adds direct messages, a navigation sidebar with shortcuts to saved posts and insights, and a cleaner single-feed layout replacing the current multi-column design. DMs, which launched on mobile in June 2025, will roll out on web “over the coming weeks,” bringing one-on-one chats, group […]
This story continues at The Next Web

items.title: Palantir posts mini-manifesto denouncing inclusivity and ‘regressive’ cultures
items.summary: Palantir's ideological bent has come under more scrutiny as it's worked with ICE and positioned itself as a defender of "the West."
